alignments.rationale: The claim of outperforming FSIS for CDR measurement is explicitly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed approach is better than those of individual graders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates better performance than individual graders in CDR measurement.
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance against graders is restated in the publication with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves performance close to the state-of-the-art obtained from FSIS and the performance of individual graders for optic cup and disc segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves dice coefficients of 0.882 and 0.950 in optic cup and disc segmentation, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete dice metrics for cup and disc segmentation, clarifying and strengthening the preprint's claim about comparable performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach achieves a cup-to-disc ratio (CDR) error of 0.0458 and an F1 score of 0.917 in CDR measurement.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specific numerical CDR error and F1 results that were not stated as claims in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated frameworks have been developed to detect and identify the stage of breast cancer using AI algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article summarizes AI and computer vision based state-of-the-art methods proposed in the last decade to detect breast cancer using various imaging modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from the assertion that automated frameworks exist (and identify stage) to summarizing methods across modalities; this clarifies the work's focus and drops explicit mention of stage identification, narrowing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The availability of labeled datasets is crucial for the development of AI-based frameworks for breast cancer detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The availability of labelled datasets is one of the most important aspects for the development of AI-based frameworks for breast cancer detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim about the central importance of labelled datasets with essentially identical meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mammograms are the most widely used imaging modality for breast cancer detection and have a significant amount of labeled datasets available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mammograms are the most widely used breast imaging modality and serve as the first test that medical practitioners usually prescribe for the detection of breast cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the point that mammograms are the most widely used modality but removes the specific claim about availability of labeled datasets and instead emphasizes clinical practice, thus revising and narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This article summarizes state-of-the-art AI and computer vision methods proposed in the last decade for breast cancer detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article summarizes AI and computer vision based state-of-the-art methods proposed in the last decade to detect breast cancer using various imaging modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the summary to explicitly include various imaging modalities (broadening) and expresses the contribution with higher certainty, clarifying and amplifying the original contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a new method for generating explanations with AI that can be tested within a user interface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach to framing illocution in a computer-friendly way improves user-centrality in the explanatory process.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the framing and user-centrality benefit but drops the explicit mention of UI testing; the claim is reframed and somewhat tempered in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach frames illocution in a computer-friendly way to achieve user-centrality with statistical question answering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach to framing illocution in a computer-friendly way improves user-centrality in the explanatory process.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core contribution—computer-friendly framing of illocution to improve user-centrality—though wording and reported certainty are slightly reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results showed that increasing the illocutionary power of an explanatory tool can produce statistically significant improvements on effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for generating explanations with AI demonstrates statistically significant improvements in effectiveness when increasing the illocutionary power of an explanatory tool.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly affirms the experimental result about statistically significant improvements in effectiveness due to increased illocutionary power with essentially the same claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a visible alignment between the increments in effectiveness and satisfaction, suggesting that our understanding of illocution can be correct.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not report or reiterate the observed alignment between effectiveness and satisfaction or the tentative conclusion about illocution, so this preprint claim does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current focus of eXplainable AI (XAI) on a One-Size-Fits-All approach fails to meet user needs in explaining Automated Decision-Making systems (ADMs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current focus of eXplainable AI (XAI) on a One-Size-Fits-All approach fails to meet user needs in explaining Automated Decision-Making systems (ADMs).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this critique verbatim; there is no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The belief that making things explainable is sufficient for pragmatic explanations is a limitation in the current XAI approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The belief that making things explainable is sufficient for pragmatic explanations limits the effectiveness of current XAI methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with equivalent meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YAI allows explainees to interactively explore an explanatory space to produce explanations tailored to their needs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end complex-valued neural network architecture eliminates the need for pooling, allowing feature extraction using large receptive fields without any loss of output resolution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation on both synthetic and real test sets demonstrated promising results across multiple energy-based metrics and perceptual proxies.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Dupex algorithm effectively identifies duplicated phrases in legal texts.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dupex leverages the Minimum Description Length principle to compress input texts by identifying patterns.

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent meaning (mathematical optimization solvers ~= traditional solvers).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The break minimization problem in an MDRRT can be expressed as a 4-regular graph.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or rephrase the graph-theoretic claim about 4-regularity, so this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum annealing was able to determine the exact solution in 0.05 seconds for problems with 20 teams.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum annealing was able to determine the exact solution in 0.05 seconds for problems with 20 teams.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative timing result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integer programming method took 84.8 seconds to reach the objective function value for 36 teams, while the quantum annealer achieved this in 0.05 seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integer programming method took 84.8 seconds to reach the objective function value, which was obtained by the quantum annealer in 0.05 seconds for 36 teams.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative timing result for 36 teams with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study contributes to identifying practical combinatorial optimization problems that can be effectively solved by quantum annealing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study contributes to finding problems that can be effectively solved by quantum annealing.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved with only minor wording differences about identifying/finding suitable problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach, CyTran, can automatically generate contrast CT scans for patients for whom injecting contrast substance is not an option.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach, CyTran, can automatically generate contrast CT scans for patients for whom injecting contrast substance is not an option.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core capability claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CyTran enhances the alignment between contrast and non-contrast CT by reducing the differences induced by the contrast substance before registration.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this specific mechanistic claim about reducing contrast-induced differences prior to registration, so the claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that CyTran outperforms all competing methods for image style transfer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CyTran outperforms all competing methods in image style transfer for CT scans.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the empirical superiority claim, with a minor clarifying mention of CT scans.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CyTran can be employed as a preliminary step to improve a state-of-the-art medical image alignment method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CyTran can be employed as a preliminary step to improve a state-of-the-art medical image alignment method.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this application-as-preprocessing conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce a novel data set, Coltea-Lung-CT-100W, containing 100 3D triphasic lung CT scans collected from 100 female patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a novel data set, Coltea-Lung-CT-100W, containing 100 3D triphasic lung CT scans.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the dataset claim but omits the demographic detail about 100 female patients, narrowing the scope of the description.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CyTran is the only approach that does not introduce visual artifacts during the translation process.
alignments.rationale: This claim about being the only artifact-free method is present in the publication but was not in the preprint, so it is an added assertive conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed network is simpler to implement compared to more complex models such as MC-Dropout or SDE-Net.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network is simpler to implement compared to more complex models such as MC-Dropout or SDE-Net.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the simplicity claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed network is more robust and insensitive to varying aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network is more robust and insensitive to varying aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty.
alignments.rationale: The robustness and insensitivity to uncertainties claim is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution extends the iterative reweighted least square (IRLS) method in generalised linear regression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution extends the iterative reweighted least square (IRLS) method in generalised linear regression.
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current map extraction techniques yield low accuracy when extended to update existing maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current map extraction techniques yield low accuracy when extended to update existing maps.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same limitation statement unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method leveraging the progression of satellite imagery over time substantially improves accuracy in updating maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel method leveraging the progression of satellite imagery over time substantially improves accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved; the publication shortens wording but retains the same meaning about temporal imagery improving accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed change-based approach reduces map update error rates four-fold.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed change-based approach reduces error rates four-fold.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative four-fold reduction claim is maintained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Scenario-Aware Ranking Network (SAR-Net) effectively addresses the challenges of training a unified model for different travel scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Scenario-Aware Ranking Network (SAR-Net) effectively addresses the challenges of training a unified model for diverse travel scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core result with synonymous phrasing (different/diverse scenarios).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAR-Net utilizes two specific attention modules to learn users' cross-scenario interests by modulating user behavior features with scenario and item features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAR-Net utilizes two specific attention modules to learn users' cross-scenario interests by modulating user behavior features with scenario and item features.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about the two attention modules is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of the Fairness Coefficient (FC) helps mitigate data fairness issues caused by manual intervention in the ranking model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of the Fairness Coefficient (FC) helps mitigate data fairness issues caused by manual intervention in the ranking model.
alignments.rationale: The fairness-mitigation contribution is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that SAR-Net outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that SAR-Net outperforms state-of-the-art methods in ranking effectiveness.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SAR-Net has been successfully deployed in Alibaba's online travel marketing platform, serving hundreds of travel scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The deployment and production-serving claim appears only in the publication and was not present in the preprint, so it is an added result-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new financial framework is presented where two families of RL-based agents learn simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new financial framework is presented where two families of RL-based agents learn simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim; no substantive change in meaning or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parametrized reward formulation and the use of Deep RL enable each group to learn a shared policy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a parametrized reward formulation and Deep RL allows each group to learn a shared policy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the result remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The shared policy is able to generalize and interpolate over a wide range of behaviors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about generalization/interpolation does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This framework is a step towards a fully RL-based market simulator that can replicate complex market conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework is a step towards a fully RL-based market simulator that can replicate complex market conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulator is particularly suited to study the dynamics of the financial market under various scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about suitability for studying market dynamics is new and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Binary Neural Networks (BNN) can be implemented and embedded on tiny restricted devices, saving a significant amount of storage, computation cost, and energy consumption. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binary Neural Networks (BNN) can be implemented and embedded on tiny restricted devices while saving a significant amount of storage, computation cost, and energy consumption. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Nearly all BNNs trade off extra memory and computation cost for higher performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This article provides a complete overview of recent developments in BNN, focusing exclusively on 1-bit activations and weights 1-bit convolution networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article provides a complete overview of recent developments in BNN, focusing exclusively on 1-bit activations and weights 1-bit convolution networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses potential directions and future research opportunities in the field of BNN. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses potential directions and future research opportunities in the field of BNN. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two top state tracking models fail to generalize well across schema variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two top state tracking models fail to generalize well across schema variants. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Joint goal accuracy and a novel metric for measuring schema sensitivity were used to evaluate the performance of the models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Joint goal accuracy and a novel metric for measuring schema sensitivity were used to evaluate the performance of the models. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple model-agnostic data augmentation method is presented to improve schema robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple model-agnostic data augmentation method is presented to improve schema robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Generative Decoder (DGD) computes model parameters and representations directly via maximum a posteriori estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DGD handles complex parameterized latent distributions naturally, unlike VAEs which typically use a fixed Gaussian distribution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DGD handles complex parameterized latent distributions naturally, unlike variational autoencoders which typically use a fixed Gaussian distribution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The DGD learns low-dimensional, meaningful, and well-structured latent representations with sub-clustering beyond the provided labels. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The DGD provides representations of much smaller dimensionality than a comparable variational autoencoder. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The multistage fusion method shows better quantitative performance compared to alternatives on most evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates the potential of multistage fusion in better assimilating text and audio information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention a comparison to RT-PCR or claim superiority of X-ray over RT-PCR, so this specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a pre-trained network, DenseNet169, and feature selection method (ANOVA) improved predictive accuracy while reducing computations and time complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the specific model/ANOVA computational/time-complexity claim, so the implementation-specific claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can predict the exact locations and forms of software defects at the requirement phase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method predicted 31.8% of distinct defect forms that may manifest in the same program.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the ability to predict defect forms but omits the stronger claim about predicting exact locations; thus the claim is narrowed to forms only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach was able to predict 31.8% of specific types of defects found in the code.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On average, 75% of the defects introduced by a programmer were predicted by the approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the predictions could have saved 46.2% of the debugging iterations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the HEDF approach could have saved an estimated 46.2% of the debugging effort.
alignments.rationale: The estimated 46.2% saving in debugging/effort is carried over directly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed learnable network extracts Meta Pattern (MP) to improve the generalization ability of face anti-spoofing systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed learnable network extracts Meta Pattern (MP) to improve the generalization ability of face anti-spoofing systems.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Replacing handcrafted features with the Meta Pattern allows for learning a more generalized model.
alignments.rationale: The result about Meta Pattern replacing handcrafted features is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-stream network with the Hierarchical Fusion Module (HFM) achieves state-of-the-art performance on two different domain generalization evaluation benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stream network with the Hierarchical Fusion Module (HFM) achieves state-of-the-art performance on two different domain generalization evaluation benchmarks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Handcrafted feature extraction relies on experts’ domain knowledge, which is a limitation in the current approaches.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed constrained trace clustering techniques leverage expert knowledge in the form of instance-level constraints.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel techniques produce clustering solutions that are more justifiable compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The result about increased justifiability is preserved without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of the clustering solutions is not substantially negatively impacted by the use of expert knowledge.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bidirectional Recurrence Network (BRN) with the optical-flow-reuse strategy improves the efficiency of space-time video super-resolution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the optical-flow-reuse-based bidirectional recurrent network (OFR-BRN) outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Taking account of AMOs when encoding PB constraints using decision diagrams can produce a dramatic improvement in solver efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Taking account of AMOs when encoding PB constraints using decision diagrams can produce a dramatic improvement in solver efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new Reduced Generalized Totalizer encoding is more compact and efficient than the popular Generalized Totalizer encoding. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Encodings of PB(AMO) constraints can be substantially smaller than those of PB constraints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Encodings of PB(AMO) constraints can be substantially smaller than those of PB constraints. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PB(AMO) encodings allow many more instances to be solved within a time limit, with solving time improved by more than one order of magnitude in some cases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficiency of each encoding may depend on PB(AMO) characteristics such as the magnitude of coefficient values. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a hybrid quantum-classical approach to generative modelling using a quantum circuit Born machine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hybrid quantum-classical approach to generative modelling using a quantum circuit born machine is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution using equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two heuristics are introduced that demonstrably improve the training of the Born machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two heuristics are introduced that demonstrably improve the training of the born machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result; the match is direct though the publication summarizes rather than listing the heuristics in this claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first heuristic is based on $f$-divergence switching during training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two heuristics are introduced that demonstrably improve the training of the born machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the f-divergence switching heuristic explicitly in a separate claim but subsumes it under the general statement about two heuristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second heuristic introduces locality to the divergence, which helps mitigate barren plateaus. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of locality to the divergence mitigates barren plateaus in training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same technical claim about locality mitigating barren plateaus with equivalent meaning and similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study generalises existing algorithms for estimating the Kullback-Leibler divergence and the total variation distance to create a fault-tolerant quantum algorithm for estimating the Pearson divergence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A fault-tolerant quantum algorithm for estimating the Pearson divergence is generalized from existing algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same contribution; publication frames it as a generalized fault-tolerant algorithm with equivalent content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognitive neuroscience has made progress in revealing mechanisms underlying pain and empathy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication reframes progress as highlighting significance for healthcare and empathic AI; core idea is preserved but the emphasis is shifted toward application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational pain recognition and empathic artificial intelligence (AI) show promise for healthcare and human-computer interaction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article aims to contribute to developing effective assistants equipped with empathic capabilities for safe interactions between humans and AI. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication places the contribution in terms of developing empathic assistants for safe interactions; this amplifies the stated application focus compared to the preprint's broader 'show promise' wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most cognitive neuroscience and AI research do not jointly address the issues of empathy in AI and cognitive neuroscience. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to this negative/limitation statement; the published claims do not explicitly state this gap.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are future challenges for artificial empathy that need to be addressed from interdisciplinary perspectives. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are associated advantages and obstacles linked with the development of empathic AI. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same notion of challenges/obstacles and advantages; wording is slightly reframed but the content aligns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Responsible evaluation of cognitive methods and computational techniques is crucial for the development of affective assistants capable of empathy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article aims to contribute to developing effective assistants equipped with empathic capabilities for safe interactions between humans and AI. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the goal of developing safe, empathic assistants; this subsumes and reframes the preprint's emphasis on responsible evaluation as part of that development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study develops an extractive title generation system that formulates titles from keywords extracted from an abstract. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extractive title generation system formulates titles from keywords extracted from an abstract. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation model can identify top-conference titles more effectively than intermediate English and beginner students. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system leads to a multi-fold increase in detection and processing throughput with minimal reduction in accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system leads to a multi-fold increase in detection and processing throughput with minimal reduction in accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep fusion prior (DFP) framework addresses the multi-focus image super resolution fusion (MFISRF) problem using a single model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed dataset-free unsupervised framework named deep fusion prior (DFP) unifies multifocus image fusion (MFIF) and super-resolution (SR) problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed 3D end-to-end Deep Multi-Task Survival model (DeepMTS) effectively integrates joint survival prediction and tumor segmentation from pretreatment PET/CT. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed 3D end-to-end Deep Multi-Task Survival model (DeepMTS) effectively integrates joint survival prediction and tumor segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the explicit 'from pretreatment PET/CT' qualifier, which broadens the claimed applicability; the core contribution is retained but the scope is less specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a hard-sharing segmentation backbone in DeepMTS reduces interference from non-relevant background information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a hard-sharing segmentation backbone in DeepMTS reduces interference from non-relevant background information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement about the hard-sharing backbone reducing background interference is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMTS captures prognostic information existing outside of primary tumors, enhancing survival prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMTS captures prognostic information existing outside of primary tumors, enhancing survival prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim that DeepMTS captures extra-tumoral prognostic information and improves accuracy is unchanged in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments with two clinical datasets demonstrate that DeepMTS consistently outperforms traditional radiomics-based survival prediction models and existing deep survival models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments with two clinical datasets demonstrate that DeepMTS consistently outperforms both traditional radiomics-based survival prediction models and existing deep survival models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority across two datasets is preserved; the publication adds the word 'both' but does not change the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fusion method for ensemble linear classifiers utilizes a probability-driven scoring function based on the orientation of decision hyperplanes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fusion method for ensemble linear classifiers utilizes a probability-driven scoring function based on the orientation of decision hyperplanes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution wording and meaning with no added qualifiers or restrictions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new fusion method is compared with a reference method using multiple benchmark datasets from the KEEL repository. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new fusion method is compared with a reference method using multiple benchmark datasets from the KEEL repository. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental comparison statement is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental study indicates that some improvement may be obtained under certain conditions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental study indicates that some improvement may be obtained under certain conditions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The cautious result about conditional improvements is carried over exactly into the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Multi-Head Feature Collaboration (MHFC) algorithm effectively addresses the distribution-shift-problem (DSP) in few-shot learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Multi-Head Feature Collaboration (MHFC) algorithm addresses the distribution-shift-problem (DSP) in few-shot learning by projecting multi-head features to a unified space and fusing them. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim that MHFC addresses DSP but adds mechanistic detail (projection and fusion), thereby clarifying how the problem is addressed without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves significant improvements of 2.1%-7.8% compared with state-of-the-art approaches on five benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of the MHFC algorithm on five benchmark datasets demonstrates significant improvements of 2.1%-7.8% compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim and evaluation scope are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a subspace learning method in the MHFC algorithm corrects the DSP by learning features with more powerful discrimination. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method corrects the DSP by learning features with more powerful discrimination and overcoming inconsistent measurement scales from different head features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the mechanism claim (learning discriminative features) and adds the overcoming of inconsistent scales; the phrasing and certainty are stronger, so the claim is clarified and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention block in the MHFC algorithm automatically updates combination weights for each head feature, improving feature discrimination. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not describe the attention block behaviour or automatic weight updates present in the preprint, so this specific contribution appears omitted in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Different feature extraction models (FEMs) emphasize various aspects of data, such as contour and texture information, which affects their performance in few-shot learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This observational conclusion about FEMs is present in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed loss function for regression uncertainty estimation is based on the Bayesian Validation Metric (BVM) framework and utilizes ensemble learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed loss function for regression uncertainty estimation is based on the Bayesian Validation Metric (BVM) framework and utilizes ensemble learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method reproduces maximum likelihood estimation in the limiting case. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reproduces maximum likelihood estimation in the limiting case. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The limiting-case behavior is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on in-distribution data show that the proposed method is competitive with existing state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on in-distribution data show that the proposed method is competitive with existing state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental in-distribution result statement is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on out-of-distribution data demonstrate that the proposed method is robust to statistical change and exhibits superior predictive capability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on out-of-distribution data demonstrate that the proposed method is robust to statistical change and exhibits superior predictive capability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The OOD robustness and superior predictive capability claim is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed lidar gesture recognition system is robust to gesture variation and suitable for mobile robot control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed lidar gesture recognition system is robust to gesture variation and suitable for mobile robot control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system's modular approach, consisting of a pose estimation module and a gesture classifier, reduces the dimensionality of the input. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system's modular approach, consisting of a pose estimation module and a gesture classifier, reduces the dimensionality of the input. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the modularity claim but strengthens its status (higher certainty and labelled as a contribution), so the claim is clarified and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of lidar allows the system to operate in most outdoor conditions and be independent of lighting conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of lidar allows the system to operate in most outdoor conditions and be independent of lighting conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The environmental robustness claim is repeated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data augmentation and automated labeling techniques minimize the amount of data collection required for the system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lidar-based pose estimator and gesture classifier utilize data augmentation and automated labeling techniques, minimizing the need for manual data collection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same point about reduced data collection but clarifies it as reducing manual data collection specifically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the system in a real-world robot teleoperation setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the system in a real-world robot teleoperation setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWAG approximates a posterior Gaussian distribution of each weight, enabling the creation of multiple models for ensemble predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SWAG approximates a posterior Gaussian distribution of each weight, enabling the creation of multiple models for ensemble predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological description of SWAG is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The standard deviation of the ensemble predictions can be used to construct confidence intervals for uncertainty quantification in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The standard deviation of the ensemble predictions can be used to construct confidence intervals for uncertainty quantification in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about using ensemble prediction variability to build CIs is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWAG-based uncertainty quantification can be applied regardless of the network architecture. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SWAG-based uncertainty quantification can be applied regardless of the neural network architecture. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The applicability across architectures is stated equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWAG can obtain physically-interpretable confidence-interval estimates from the perspective of model-form uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SWAG can obtain physically-interpretable confidence-interval estimates from the perspective of epistemic uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'model-form uncertainty' with the more standard term 'epistemic uncertainty', clarifying the interpretation while retaining the same substantive claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The applicability of SWAG is demonstrated for various neural network types and complex datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This demonstration claim about applicability appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decorrelating uncertainties significantly reduces the apparent uncertainty while the actual uncertainty is much larger.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The true uncertainty is much larger than the apparent uncertainty when using decorrelation techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's core point that the true uncertainty is much larger; wording is split across two pub claims but the substantive claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Caution should be taken when using decorrelation for theory uncertainties that do not have a statistical origin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Caution should be taken when using decorrelation for theory uncertainties without a complete decomposition into statistically meaningful components.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the caution more specific by requiring a complete decomposition into statistical components, increasing certainty in the recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explicit examples of two-point and continuous uncertainties demonstrate the limitations of decorrelation in uncertainty estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the explicit example cases called out in the preprint; the illustrative examples appear to have been omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Decorrelating uncertainties significantly reduces the apparent uncertainty in certain cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a more qualified statement ('in certain cases') about reduction of apparent uncertainty that was not separately present as a standalone claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel initialization-free primal-dual method converges to the global optimum for rotation averaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel initialization-free primal-dual method converges to the global optimum for rotation averaging.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first optimal closed-form solution for rotation averaging in cycle graphs has been derived.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents the first optimal closed-form solution for rotation averaging in cycle graphs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve a significant gain in both precision and performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods achieve a significant gain in both precision and performance.
alignments.rationale: Results claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our text-only model outperforms pretrained multimodal (image-text) models of comparable number of parameters on a visual question answering task requiring external knowledge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A text-only model outperforms pretrained multimodal (image-text) models of comparable number of parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our text-only method is especially effective for tasks requiring external knowledge, as indicated by its lower effectiveness in a standard VQA task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The text-only method is especially effective for tasks requiring external knowledge, but less effective in standard VQA tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing the language model's size notably improves its performance, yielding results comparable to the state-of-the-art with our largest model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method attains state-of-the-art results when increasing the size of the language model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic captions often fail to capture relevant information in the images, which is balanced by the better inference ability of the text-only language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The text-only language models demonstrate better inference ability despite automatic captions often failing to capture relevant information.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the observation about captions failing and the models' stronger inference ability; wording is slightly rephrased but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work opens up possibilities to further improve inference in visio-linguistic tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work opens up possibilities to further improve inference in visio-linguistic tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network's ability to anticipate the systematic differences between low and high resolution model dynamics explains the enhanced performance of SRDA.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SRDA reduces the errors by 40%, making the performance very close to the high-resolution system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: With a 25-member ensemble at low resolution, SRDA achieves a 40% reduction in errors while only incurring a 55% computational overhead.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 40% error reduction result and adds experimental details (ensemble size and computational overhead), clarifying the original numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reliability of the ensemble system is not degraded by SRDA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reliability of the ensemble system is not degraded by SRDA.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the reliability conclusion exactly.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Consistency regularization is introduced to ensure that the representation of original images and their transformations are consistent.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach performs on-par with completely supervised methods using only a fraction of labeled examples.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimentation on the dataset shows good performance on test set images for Urdu text extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel approach assigns vertices to bone-influenced clusters and derives weights using deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal set of transformation and vertices is derived in fewer iterations due to better initial positioning in the multidimensional variable space. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method requires no parameters to be determined or tuned by the user during the compression process. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method requires no parameters to be determined or tuned by the user during the compression process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed segmentation method segments egocentric images in terms of how a robot can navigate in them. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed segmentation method segments egocentric images in terms of how a robot can navigate in them. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a generic affordance consisting of 3 driveability levels improves segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating inter-class distances during learning enhances segmentation compared to standard one-hot labeling. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating inter-class distances during learning improves segmentation compared to standard hard one-hot labeling. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The navigation-oriented pixel-wise loss weighting method assigns higher importance to safety-critical areas. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed one-dimensional generative model can predict the number of photons detected by particular photon detectors with the same level of detail as the Geant4 simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
