alignments.rationale: Contribution claim is carried forward verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that the spiking neural network provides near real-time performance in realistic applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate that the spiking neural network provides near real-time performance in realistic applications.
alignments.rationale: Performance/conclusion claim unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QUINT provides tremendous gain in terms of speed and space usage without compromising much on the accuracy of the downstream tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QUINT provides tremendous gain in terms of speed and space usage without compromising much on the accuracy of the downstream tasks.
alignments.rationale: High-level performance claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QUINT achieves a speedup of up to 7000x and space saving of up to 80x due to its bit-wise nature to obtain node embedding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QUINT achieves a speedup of up to 7000 times and space saving of up to 800 times due to its bit-wise nature to obtain node embedding.
alignments.rationale: The numeric space-saving figure changed substantially between preprint (80x) and publication (800x), increasing the claimed benefit; speedup figure remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QUINT is a consistent top-performer for link prediction and node classification tasks among the baselines across all datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QUINT is a consistent top-performer for link prediction and node classification tasks among the baselines across all datasets.
alignments.rationale: Performance ranking claim preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QUINT retains enough structural information which can be used to approximate many topological properties of networks with high confidence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QUINT retains enough structural information which can be used to approximate many topological properties of networks with high confidence.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion about retained structural information is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised hypergraph learning framework effectively captures the intra- and inter-group interactions among users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised hypergraph learning framework effectively captures the intra- and inter-group interactions among users.
alignments.rationale: Claim preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework alleviates the data sparsity issue using the raw data itself.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework alleviates the data sparsity issue using the raw data itself.
alignments.rationale: Same claim about sparsity mitigation present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hierarchical hypergraph convolutional network is developed to model complex tuplewise correlations among users within and beyond groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hierarchical hypergraph convolutional network is developed to model complex tuplewise correlations among users.
alignments.rationale: Publication wording removes the phrase 'within and beyond groups,' slightly narrowing the stated scope while keeping the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental analysis demonstrates the superiority of the proposed model over existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental analysis demonstrates the superiority of the proposed model over existing methods.
alignments.rationale: Result claim retained without substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The double-scale node dropout strategy creates self-supervision signals that regularize user representations against the sparsity issue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The double-scale node dropout strategy creates self-supervision signals that regularize user representations against the sparsity issue.
alignments.rationale: Contribution claim preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for generating interactive explanations in AI demonstrates a statistically relevant improvement in effectiveness over a baseline tool.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for generating interactive explanations in AI demonstrates a statistically relevant improvement in effectiveness over a baseline static explanatory tool.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the baseline as 'static explanatory tool' but the substantive claim of statistical improvement over a baseline remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Achinstein's Theory of Explanation can be adapted for implementation into a concrete software application for interactive question answering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Achinstein’s Theory of Explanation can be adapted for implementation into a concrete software application for interactive question answering.
alignments.rationale: Conceptual claim unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach focuses on handling generic questions as preliminary overviews rather than as answers to explicit questions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interactive explanations generated by the proposed method focus on providing overviews rather than answering explicit questions.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the same idea (focus on overviews vs. explicit answers) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The user study involved more than 100 participants and provided evidence in favor of the proposed theory.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about a >100-participant user study; the preprint-specific study claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms, Compositional Affinity Propagation (CAP), Compositional k-means (CKM), and Greedy Compositional Reassignment (GCR), can effectively partition examples into coherent groups and infer the compositional structure among them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms, Compositional Affinity Propagation (CAP), Compositional k-means (CKM), and Greedy Compositional Reassignment (GCR), can effectively partition examples into coherent groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the explicit claim about inferring compositional structure while keeping the core claim about partitioning into coherent groups, so the claim is narrowed/revised rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel clustering task focuses on finding compositional clusters that represent the union of the properties of constituent clusters, differing from traditional hierarchical clustering. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new clustering task focuses on finding compositional clusters that represent the union of properties of constituent clusters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core definition of the task but omits the explicit contrast with hierarchical clustering, making the statement slightly streamlined/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms demonstrate promising results compared to popular algorithms such as Gaussian mixtures, Fuzzy c-means, and Agglomerative Clustering on the OmniGlot and LibriSpeech datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms demonstrate promising results compared to popular clustering algorithms such as Gaussian mixtures, Fuzzy c-means, and Agglomerative Clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same comparative performance claim (dataset mention omitted in the shorter pub text) without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work has applications in open-world multi-label object recognition and speaker identification & diarization with simultaneous speech from multiple speakers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work has applications in open-world multi-label object recognition and speaker identification & diarization with simultaneous speech from multiple speakers. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The application claims are essentially identical between preprint and publication, with only minor metadata/type wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of positive labels in HOI classes leads to low classification accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of positive labels in HOI classes leads to low classification accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural correlations and anti-correlations among human-object interactions can be modeled as action co-occurrence matrices. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural correlations and anti-correlations among human-object interactions can be modeled as action co-occurrence matrices. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed techniques for learning priors improve training effectiveness, particularly for rare classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Techniques to learn and leverage action co-occurrence matrices lead to more effective training on rare HOI classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about improved training for rare classes using learned priors/co-occurrence matrices.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed approach consistently improves over state-of-the-art methods on HICO-Det and V-COCO datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach consistently improves performance over state-of-the-art methods on the HICO-Det and V-COCO datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical performance claim is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model, TrAISformer, significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in vessel trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model, TrAISformer, significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in vessel trajectory prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TrAISformer achieves an average prediction performance below 10 nautical miles for vessel positions up to ~10 hours ahead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TrAISformer achieves an average prediction performance below 10 nautical miles for vessel positions up to ~10 hours ahead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantitative performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a discrete, high-dimensional representation of AIS data and a new loss function effectively addresses the challenges of heterogeneity and multimodality in motion data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a discrete, high-dimensional representation of AIS data and a new loss function addresses the challenges of heterogeneity and multimodality in vessel trajectory prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is preserved with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NU:BRIEF is a web application that allows publishers to personalize their newsletters without harvesting personal data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NU:BRIEF is a web application that allows publishers to personalize their newsletters without harvesting personal data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Personalized newsletters build a habit and serve as an effective conversion tool for publishers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Personalized newsletters can build a habit among readers and serve as an effective conversion tool for publishers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the assertion by adding 'can', reducing confidence/strength though retaining the same claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of personalized newsletters provides an alternative revenue model for publishers compared to the declining ad/clickbait-centered business model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of personalized newsletters provides an alternative revenue model for publishers, moving away from a declining ad/clickbait-centered business model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about alternative revenue models is preserved with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QD-Meta archives provide improved average performance and faster adaptation to a priori unknown changes to the environment when compared to CVT-MAP-Elites and AURORA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QD-Meta archives provide improved average performance and faster adaptation to a priori unknown changes to the environment when compared to CVT-MAP-Elites and AURORA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical comparison claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QD-Meta evolves a population of QD algorithms to optimise the algorithms and their resulting archives to a user-defined meta-objective. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting archives from QD-Meta are tailored to the meta-objectives provided by the end user. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the outcome (tailored archives) rather than the evolutionary process; the core intent is preserved but phrased to highlight result, a clarification of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There remains a need for customising behavioural diversity to a particular meta-objective specified by the end-user. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There remains a need for customizing behavioral diversity to a particular meta-objective specified by the end user. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is retained with only minor spelling/formatting changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research finds an easy way out of the bottleneck of memory and processing power in IoT devices by depreciating the need for feature engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research finds an easy way out of the bottleneck of memory and processing power in IoT devices by depreciating the need for feature engineering.
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same between preprint and publication, but the reported certainty was reduced from high to medium in the publication, so the meaning is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a Featureless machine learning process to perform anomaly detection using unprocessed byte streams of packets as training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a Featureless machine learning process to perform anomaly detection using unprocessed byte streams of packets as training data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in both versions with the same high certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Featureless machine learning enables a low cost and low memory time-series analysis of network traffic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Featureless machine learning enables a low cost and low memory time-series analysis of network traffic.
alignments.rationale: The claim wording is identical but the publication reports higher certainty (medium -> high), so the claim is unchanged semantically but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach benefits from eliminating the significant investment in subject matter experts and the time required for feature engineering.
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about reducing investment in experts/time is present in the preprint but does not appear among the publication claims, so it was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning (FL) allows clients to collaboratively train a machine learning model without revealing their private training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated learning (FL) allows clients to collaboratively train a machine learning model without revealing their private training data.
alignments.rationale: The definitional contribution is identical in preprint and publication with the same high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The goal of FL is to minimize the time-to-accuracy of model training while maintaining an acceptable level of privacy guarantee.
alignments.rationale: The stated goal about minimizing time-to-accuracy while maintaining privacy appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication claims, so it was omitted in the final claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are four distinct challenges to achieving short time-to-accuracy in FL training: lack of information for optimization, tradeoff between statistical and system utility, client heterogeneity, and large configuration space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are four distinct challenges to achieving short time-to-accuracy in FL training: lack of information for optimization, tradeoff between statistical and system utility, client heterogeneity, and large configuration space.
alignments.rationale: The list of four challenges is carried over verbatim to the publication; its characterization changed to 'limitation' in metadata but the substantive content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper surveys recent works addressing the challenges of FL and presents them through a typical training workflow.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper surveys recent works addressing the challenges of FL and presents them through a typical training workflow.
alignments.rationale: The survey contribution is present and unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Popularity Adjusted Block Model (PABM) is a special case of the Generalized Random Dot Product Graph (GRDPG).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Popularity Adjusted Block Model (PABM) is a special case of the Generalized Random Dot Product Graph (GRDPG) where communities correspond to mutually orthogonal subspaces of latent vectors.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same core claim but adds a precise characterization (mutually orthogonal subspaces), so the claim is clarified without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New algorithms for community detection and parameter estimation for the PABM can be constructed based on the connection to the GRDPG.
alignments.pub_claim_text: New algorithms for community detection and parameter estimation for the PABM can be constructed based on the connection to the GRDPG.
alignments.rationale: The contribution linking PABM algorithm construction to GRDPG is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absolute number of community detection errors tends to zero as the number of graph vertices tends to infinity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The absolute number of community detection errors tends to zero as the number of graph vertices tends to infinity.
alignments.rationale: The asymptotic result is identical between preprint and publication with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural-IMLS is a novel approach that directly learns the noise-resistant signed distance function from unoriented raw point clouds in a self-supervised fashion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural-IMLS directly learns a noise-resistant signed distance function from unoriented raw point clouds in a self-supervised manner.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same in both versions with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutual learning mechanism between the MLP and the IMLS produces a faithful signed distance function whose zero-level set approximates the underlying surface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: At convergence, the neural network produces a faithful signed distance function whose zero-level set approximates the underlying surface.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the mechanism-focused preprint statement to emphasize the network's behavior at convergence, adding clarity and slightly changing emphasis but not the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural-IMLS can reconstruct faithful shapes on various benchmarks with noise and missing parts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural-IMLS can reconstruct faithful shapes even with noise and missing parts.
alignments.rationale: The empirical reconstruction claim is effectively identical in both versions and retained with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CMFD scheme can achieve better detection performance under various attacks compared with other state-of-the-art CMFD schemes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed CMFD scheme can achieve better detection performance under various attacks compared with other state-of-the-art CMFD schemes.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is the same in both preprint and publication with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolving circular domains coverage (ECDC) algorithm improves the accuracy of copy-move forgery detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolving circular domains coverage (ECDC) algorithm improves the accuracy of copy-move forgery detection.
alignments.rationale: The contribution regarding the ECDC algorithm is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of block-based and keypoint-based forgery detection methods enhances the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of block-based and keypoint-based forgery detection methods enhances the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.
alignments.rationale: The integrative conclusion appears identically in both versions with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method using ANFIS-BS achieved an accuracy of 99.74% in classifying EEG signals into two classes on the Bonn dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ANFIS-BS method achieved an accuracy of 99.74% in classifying EEG signals into two classes on the Bonn dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and numeric result are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ANFIS-BS method also achieved an accuracy of 99.46% in ternary classification on the Bonn dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ANFIS-BS method also achieved an accuracy of 99.46% in ternary classification on the Bonn dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same reported metric and dataset in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method reached an accuracy of 99.28% on the Freiburg dataset, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reached an accuracy of 99.28% on the Freiburg dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Accuracy value is the same but the publication omits the preprint's additional assertion of 'state-of-the-art' performance, reducing the claim's strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of tunable-Q wavelet transform (TQWT) allows for the decomposition of EEG signals into different sub-bands for feature extraction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding discussion of TQWT-based decomposition appears in the set of publication claims; the claim is absent in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of fuzzy theory and deep learning techniques in the diagnostic procedure enhances the classification performance of EEG signals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about fuzzy theory and deep learning enhancing performance is present in the publication but was not in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signal fluctuation in indoor positioning is highly location-dependent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signal fluctuation in indoor positioning is highly location-dependent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signal uncertainty is a critical yet commonly overlooked dimension of the radio signal to be fingerprinted. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signal uncertainty is a critical yet commonly overlooked dimension of the radio signal to be fingerprinted. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conceptual claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Gaussian Processes (DGP) are proposed as a more informative alternative to model signal fingerprint uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Gaussian Processes (DGP) are proposed as a more informative alternative to model signal fingerprint uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of DGP in measuring uncertainty in signal fingerprinting is evaluated using simulated and realistically collected datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of DGP in measuring uncertainty in signal fingerprinting is evaluated using simulated and realistically collected datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Evaluation claim matches exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FMI.jl library enables the integration of FMI into the Julia programming environment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FMI.jl library enables the integration of FMI into the Julia programming environment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Direct match of the library contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FMIFlux.jl extends FMI.jl by allowing the integration of FMUs into a neural network topology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FMIFlux.jl extends FMI.jl by allowing the integration of FMUs into a neural network topology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution described in both.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of a black-box model and a data-driven machine learning model in the NeuralFMU provides advantages of both modeling approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of a black-box model and a data-driven machine learning model in the NeuralFMU provides advantages of both modeling approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conceptual conclusion appears in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Advanced data-driven modeling techniques can be applied to physical effects that are difficult to model based on first principles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Advanced data-driven modeling techniques can be applied to physical effects that are difficult to model based on first principles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this general conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contemporary domain adaptation methods for cross-domain image understanding perform poorly when the source domain is noisy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contemporary domain adaptation methods for cross-domain image understanding perform poorly when the source domain is noisy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The paper retains the same diagnostic result about existing methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Noise Tolerant Domain Adaptation, effectively identifies noisy source data using a Gaussian mixture model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Noise Tolerant Domain Adaptation (NTDA) method incorporates a Gaussian mixture noise model for unsupervised noise removal. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the Gaussian mixture usage as an unsupervised noise-removal component; this clarifies the mechanism relative to the preprint's emphasis on identification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel cluster-level adversarial adaptation method aligns unlabeled target data with less noisy class prototypes to improve semantic structure mapping across domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No explicit publication claim describes the cluster-level adversarial alignment mechanism present in the preprint's claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art WSDA methods on both general images and medical images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NTDA method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art Weakly Supervised Domain Adaptation (WSDA) methods in extensive experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim of significantly outperforming WSDA methods is preserved in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Prior methods on WSDA have the problem of class misalignment, where features of different classes may be incorrectly mapped in the embedding space. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This explicit articulation of class misalignment as a limitation appears in the publication but was not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The traditional majority vote annotation process in supervised machine learning is often problematic. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The traditional majority vote method of data annotation in Machine Learning is often problematic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same critique of majority-vote annotation; wording differs only slightly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data perspectivism offers a new paradigm that integrates the opinions and perspectives of human subjects in the knowledge representation step of machine learning processes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed perspectivism paradigm advocates for preserving divergence of opinions in the ground truthing process of Machine Learning development. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the contribution toward preserving divergent opinions in ground-truthing rather than the broader wording about integration in knowledge representation, clarifying focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed perspectivist stance in machine learning has potential advantages for both subjective tasks, such as those related to human language, and objective tasks, such as medical decision making. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adopting a perspectivist stance in Machine Learning can benefit both subjective tasks and those commonly understood as objective. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The benefit-for-both-subjective-and-objective-tasks claim is preserved with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article presents possible disadvantages of adopting a perspectivist stance in machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the discussion of possible disadvantages as an explicit claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of recommendations and a research agenda are outlined to advance the perspectivist stance in machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article outlines a research agenda to advance the perspectivist stance in Machine Learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the research agenda but omits explicit mention of a separate set of recommendations, narrowing the original combined claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Discriminatory Auto-Encoder (DAE) demonstrates better results in both accuracy and speed of detection compared to three anomaly detection models from the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Contextual Auto-Encoder (CAE) model demonstrates better results in both accuracy and speed of detection compared to three anomaly detection models from the literature.
alignments.rationale: The core result (better accuracy and speed versus three models) is retained, but the model name/description changed from DAE to CAE, so the claim is revised rather than identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DAE is a novel multivariate anomaly detection model that utilizes multiple decoders for different flight phases during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CAE model is based on a regular LSTM-based auto-encoder with several decoders that process data from specific flight phases during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the multiple-decoder / flight-phase idea but adds an explicit LSTM-based AE description (clarification); the certainty is reduced in the pub text (medium vs high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset created for evaluating the DAE includes both real-life anomalies and realistically crafted anomalies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset created for evaluating the CAE includes real-life anomalies and realistically crafted trajectory modifications.
alignments.rationale: The dataset description is retained but wording is refined to 'trajectory modifications'; the publication expresses higher certainty about the dataset composition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The availability of the dataset, model implementations, and evaluation results in an online repository facilitates replicability and future experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The availability of the dataset, model implementations, and evaluation results in an online repository facilitates replicability and future experiments.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in both versions; the publication states it with higher confidence (medium -> high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel Entropy-Constrained and XAI-adjusted Quantization (ECQ$^{\text{x}}$) method generates ultra low-precision (2-5 bit) and simultaneously sparse neural networks while maintaining or even improving model performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel Entropy-Constrained and XAI-adjusted Quantization (ECQx) method generates ultra low-precision (2–5 bit) and simultaneously sparse neural networks while maintaining or even improving model performance.
alignments.rationale: The core claim and scope are the same; differences are formatting/name normalization only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ECQ$^{\text{x}}$ method preserves the most relevant weights in quantization clusters of highest information content.
